attacking_style: Does not initiate attacks; excels in uphill sprint finishes (sub-1km) after breaks are caught, exploiting positioning when favorites mark each other or underestimate him.
team_tactics: Relies heavily on team (e.g., UAE teammates) to control tempo and drive pace on final climbs, while Williams conserves energy and follows wheels until sprint finish.
terrain_preference: Punchy uphill finishes and technical circuits where bike-handling and positioning trump pure climbing. Vulnerable when sustained tempo increases on Cat 2+ climbs.
vulnerability: Exposed when sustained climbing tempo increases; briefly dropped on Cat 2 climbs before recovering. Struggles to defend race leads through personal effort on harder mountain stages.
key_patterns: Won uphill sprints at both Tour de Suisse and Tour de Pologne (2 wins observed)
Conserves energy deliberately, follows wheels, contributes minimal chase effort even when holding race lead
Succeeds via superior positioning in sprint chaos while favorites mark each other (2 instances)
Defended stage race GC through team tactics and bike-handling, not climbing strength (3 stages)
Vulnerable on sustained climbs but recovers over summit when pace stabilizes (2 instances)
form_trajectory: positive: Won first WorldTour race at 26, suggesting late development curve; demonstrated tactical maturity and converted opportunities when positioned correctly across June-August 2022
